text:
In a forest clearing bathed in sunshine, an adventurous old owl named Olga perched high in a bauble of twisted branches. "Today," she whispered to no one in particular, "I shall find the enchanted moonbeams." She fluttered down through a thicket toward a soft rustle among the leaves.

A tiny, curious kitsake popped out. "Really?" she hooted. "Where?" It waved its paw leafily.

"To the moon! Right now!" With fluttering enthusiasm, she flapped away. Hours flew by. She danced around mushrooms, snatched dewdrops from blades of grass, and sung songs made up on the spot.

Just as Olga was about to swoop down--

"Augh! Something large!" She screeched. Out of the brush came Big Bert, a bear as big as an old car.

"Bear!" Olga screeched and took off, leaving behind dewy dewdrops.

On landing beside a pond,
